question: A team is preparing to deploy multiple DMN files to Camunda 8.8. Per the modeler reference, what rule governs the `id` attribute on the `definitions` element of each DRG?

options.text: Each DRG should have a unique ID when deployed to Camunda.
All DRGs in the same project must share an ID equal to the project key.
The DRG ID must equal the file name without the `.dmn` extension.
The DRG ID is auto-generated by the engine when omitted, so uniqueness is guaranteed.

a.text: The reference states the ID is the technical identifier of the DRG (the `id` on `definitions`) and that each DRG should have a unique ID when deployed to Camunda.

b.text: There is no rule that DRGs in a project must share an ID; the rule is the opposite — each DRG should have a unique ID.

c.text: The DRG ID is the `id` attribute on `definitions` and is independent of the file name.

d.text: The DRG ID is authored on the `definitions` element; it is not auto-generated by the engine on deployment.

explanation: The DRG ID is the technical identifier set in the `id` attribute on the `definitions` element of the DMN file. Each DRG should have a unique ID when deployed to Camunda so the engine can distinguish them.
